sentences.text: Good morning, Katie, Alicia, how are y'all?
Hey, good morning, how are ya?
Good morning!
Doing good, doing good.
Did you guys have a good weekend?
Yes, how about you?
Yeah, mine was… pretty good.
No complaints on my end.
Nice.
Do this one?
Just enjoyed the sun.
I mean, it's the best thing to do in San Diego, hit the beach.
How about you?
Yeah, for sure.
I just, went to a mountain range out here, did some trails with some friends.
Awesome.
Cool, hiking, or…
Some trail running.
I'm gonna go hike, up in Flagstaff, Saturday.
We're gonna do Humphreys Peak, which is, like, the highest, point, so…
Hope you're ready!
Oh, yeah, yeah.
100 degrees, or what?
No, not in Flagstaff.
It'll be, like, in the 70s.
Oh, nice, okay.
Yeah, that'll be cool.
Alyssa, what'd you get into?
Oh my goodness, this weekend I did a lot of nothing, which was intentional and awesome.
We've been, like, go, go, go for the last few weeks, and this weekend did not have plans, so took advantage of that.
Nice.
Yeah, sometimes those are, those are good, like, mental resets, or even just, like, body resets.
Well, and I, like, didn't even realize how physically exhausting it was.
It was nice to just lounge.
Love that, it's great.
I am forwarding this invite to Riley real quick.
She should be joining any sec.
Awesome.
She's… she's on the marketing team, and I think it'll be helpful for her to…
Cool.
Cool, so just to kind of, Hunter, thank you.

We did one little demo prior to this, And I definitely wanted to loop the marketing team in before we make any decisions, just to kind of get their take on it, so thank you for bearing with me and kind of hosting us twice, but… See if she has any other questions that I didn't think of.
Yeah, I think that makes sense.
Should we, do you want to get started, or do you want to wait real quick?
We can get started.
She should be jumping in any sec, but…
Awesome.
And then, I guess, just so I can kind of know where to put, like, kind of play color here on the call, Katie, how much did you tell Alicia about ApartmentIQ and what we can do?
Otherwise, I can…
Yeah, so I guess she does have some experience, because of… she works with Cloud 10.

So I think she has a general kind of understanding of the platform, but if you can maybe walk us through The dashboard, where it pulls information from, and then we can kind of see what questions Tough up from there.
Yeah, that sounds good.
I'll give you the, I'll give you the quick rundown, Alicia, but we have been around for the last 5 years, and we have been tracking, publicly available market data all the way down to the unit level.

So, our platform is unique in that any sort of data point, or, like, KPI, or metric, or any sort of, like, unit floor plan positioning that we're pulling, if you're ever, like, where is this coming from?
We can trace it directly back to the source, and we can show you the exact insights into whatever sort of data point that is.
Here's Riley, let them in.
Within the platform, we have 3 different functionalities.

So, one of which is great for the day-to-day operations, and what that is going to be is a live data feed into your owned or managed assets, and then each specific comp set that you're tracking for each.
So, the nice thing about that is, depending on who's taking a look at the data, what sort of picture you're trying to paint.

You do look at it live, but we give you the ability to aggregate that data back over the last 5 years for, like, seasonality and trends, so you could look at it week over week, month over month, quarter over quarter.
or live.
And that is great because you get to see where each individual unit and floor plan is positioned within each, specific comp set that you're tracking.
Second functionality is, great for acquisitions or new development.
You basically get to query our entire database within a Corbin IQ.
All you need is the target asset name or address, and then you can spin up these real-time market surveys in a matter of, like, 3 seconds.
The way this works is our system will pull in what we believe to be the top 10 comps for that subject property.
But much like, whether it's your own asset or a target property, you and your teams are in complete control of the comp sets.
So there's a functionality I'll walk you through where you can edit your comps as often as you'd like.
You can add as many or remove as many as you'd like.
And then the last functionality in the platform is going to be very similar to CoStar.
It is all market, sub-market level information.
You can create custom boundaries, different mile radiuses, take it all the way down to the neighborhood.

But you'll get market sub-market level information there, you'll get new supply data for construction pipeline, different stages that it's in, and then demographic information for that market, just to help you better understand your renter profiles.

So… I would say maybe, like, 2 years ago, we just used to be, like, an automated market survey tool, and then with some of the partners that we've landed in the space, they pretty much came to us and said, hey, we have our hands tied in, like, so many different data providers, we don't love all of them throughout.
There's bits and pieces that we use.
Could you just help us kind of marry a data source together?
And that's essentially what we've done.
Sweet.
Yeah, I, So, Riley and I are part of the PSG team, which is basically, we are the marketing support for Sunrise and Cloud 10, as Katie mentioned.
And so, Cloud 10 has rolled out Apartment IQ, mainly in our Pacific Northwest market.
And that was led primarily from operations leaders, and so we were tagged in, but not necessarily part of this, like, initial discovery phase process.

When it comes to Sunrise, though, I will say one thing that we do sometimes find challenging with understanding comps, we have a division called Urban Properties, and I don't know, Katie, if you already shared this with Hunter, but… I'm kind of curious, because urban properties are small properties, and a lot of them don't have websites, they're on Appfolio, so it's just kind of a different… scenario than what we've already rolled out, which is large properties on Entrata, and that do all have websites, and a lot of times ILSs and things like that.
So, I'm just curious, like I said, on It… how that… how that process is when it comes to small property comparisons when there's not as much available online.
Sure.

So, the nice thing about our platform is, doesn't matter what property management software you're on, so it could be Appfolio, Resman, Entrata, RealPage, etc, because it's a standalone login, we don't actually integrate.
And then the nice thing about that, too, is we can essentially track any property in the States.
They don't have to be our customer.
The only caveat to that, and you Just kind of touched on that, is they have to have an online presence.

So all the data that we're pulling is being fed directly from the online leasing section of the property websites, and the reason being is because usually what's being fed through the online leasing section is being pushed live through the PMS software.
Or, we can go to, like, sitemap and, things like that.
We typically stay away from ILSs, But that's kind of how that works.
So, to your point, it doesn't matter, like, how small an asset is, if, you know, they don't have that many units, they just have to have an online presence.
Otherwise, there's nothing for us to kind of pull into the system.
So if you don't… if you don't necessarily, like, scrape ILSs, then it means their online presence… presence, excuse me, has to be a website.
Correct.
Because with our small properties, like, a lot of times they don't have individual websites.

We have a collective website for some, and then they do, though, will have… they will have their individual ILSs at times when needed, but they don't have websites, so I'm just, it's not a… it's not a, like.

hard stop on that, but I'm just saying it's one… it's one of the challenges that I perceive potentially being where if we're trying to assess, like, a smaller property… and Katie, you might be able to speak to this even more than I do, because you're in it on a daily basis more than I am when it comes to our small properties, but do you ever, like, need to find comps on those properties as well?
Oh, yeah, for sure.
I haven't been involved in the urban as much, but, I mean, I would be curious, maybe, if you could test it with one.
Yeah, do you have.
is, like, 4748 Kansas Street.

And then, I guess real quick, too, the reason we don't typically go to ILSs is because they have, like, capped availability, and if you don't get availability right, then it kind of screws up all the other metrics.
Yeah.
And the nice thing is, with our scrapers, we have, like, 500 or 600 of them, and they've gotten pretty sophisticated, so when you actually go to, like, the online leasing section, or, like, sitemap.

you can actually uncover hidden availability directly through that, just by moving around, move-in dates, typing in exact unit numbers, because we've actually seen, like, every single unit come and go from all these assets, so you can type those in, and it'll pop up.
With the online… with the ILSs, you can't do that.
So, I mean, we could still get you the data through, like, an ILS.

If there's a… if there's a property that doesn't have a presence, we would probably just mark it with, like, a limited data tag, which is something that we do where if we, for some reason, can't get around hidden availability, or if we think that they're not showing all their availability or anything like that, we will mark it as, like, hey, like, this'll point you in the right direction, but something is definitely off here Just a heads up with that.
That's good.
Any… other questions top of mind?
Otherwise, I'll share my screen, I'll walk you guys through it.
I have, more of a comment.
So, I… I believe we've… put this in as a feature request on the Cloud 10 side, but it would probably… well, it would apply with this as well.

Is there ever gonna be an option where… so, like, with CoStar, for example, when we pull market comps in CoStar, we can see what the comps are on as far as their package level, which helps us assess, like.
Okay, if we're seeing that, you know, out of the 10 comps, 5 are on higher levels, then for us to even be competitive, we need to also be on a higher level.
So with that, I didn't know if ApartmentIQ would ever have the ability to say these comps are on Zillow and or Apartments.
com.
I know maybe not the level of package, but at least, like.
Kind of a… a snapshot of what they're utilizing in their online presence.

see where your head is there.

historicals, you get 5 years worth of those to leverage for seasonality and trends.

You can actually, toggle on or off what's gonna be most important to you.

If we click on this, two different ways to take a look at this.

The right-hand view is going to be a map view, where the blue pin, again, is your property, the white pin are comps in the set, black pins are gonna be comps that maybe you want to take a look and add.
It's as easy as hovering over and clicking Add or Remove.
The property will either get added to the comp set with the 5 years of historicals, or removed.
The left-hand side is a little bit more analytical approach, where we're gonna grade all these assets with a comp score, and list them out from high to low.
That is going to be based off of distance to your property, year bill to renovation, total units, rent matching, property type.
class… what class it is, and then amenity matching, and that's how we spit out these scores.
I always say, boots on the ground, you know your market best.
This is just a AI algorithmetic, way to kind of suggest who, potentially, you maybe want to take a second look at.
So it's as simple as clicking add or remove.

The nice thing is, is not all of these properties will be relevant to yours, so we give you every single filter here, that you want to manipulate all this data to show exactly the properties that are going to be best fits for you.
Any sort of questions here?
Okay, so if we go back to the market survey, the way this works, it's broken up into four sections.
First one's gonna be property summary.
This is all high-level property data.
We'll show you things like property class, total units, management company, link you to the Google reviews.
We'll show you both gross rent, price per square foot, net effective, net effective price per square foot.
We do track concessions every single day as well.
Doesn't matter if it's a ribbon, pop-up, banner, text.
And then what we do is we use a large language model, captures all of that, inputs it into the application, and then does the concession calculation on the floor plan, bed type, and or unit.

I think something else that is nice here is when we get down to some of the calculated metrics, things like occupancy, leased exposure, even the availability, applications, cancels, or leases, the nice thing is, is again, you can drill down into any of these metrics and see exactly what units that we're referencing.
So, if we take a look at, like, Candy Music Road, there's 44 units currently available.
If you click into it, we'll show you all those, 44 units.
Another thing that's nice is when you're keeping track of, like, applications, leases or cancels, again, you can click into any of them.
So, we're staying over the last 7 days, Canon Music Grows had 3 apps.
If you click into here, we'll show you those exact units.
And then what I like is you could even take it a step further.
For any floor plan or unit, we'll give you the pricing history.
So if we click into the A9 here.
We will show you, over time, both the gross rent, net effective, and the concession amount.
And then just know any sort of gap in the data you'd see here just means that none of the A9s were available.
And you could take this down to the unit level if you'd like.
So we try to be as transparent as possible.

And the way that this works is, if a unit's on our website today, and it leaves tomorrow, instead of just counting that as a lease, we will actually count that as an application, and if that unit stays offline for the next 7 days, we will then move it to a lease.
If it comes back online in the 7-day period, we will then count that as a cancel or denial.
Any sort of questions there?
Question.
So, we do sometimes all of our… all of our availability is not always all displayed.
In my opinion, I think it should always be displayed, but a lot of times, our clients or our operations teams prefer to do, like, a max number of units available, even though we have more.

then each individual property also, too, just so you have, like, the context, each individual property is billed separately, too.
